text:
Memo - I'm Not That Smart, But I'm Not Going To Make The Same Mistake Twice

Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, shame on me. Valuable lessons to take away from the asinine May to July oil price action.

Free subscribers can use the one-time unlock feature.

https://t.co/1jKRZRSH8Y

Strait of Hormuz over the last 72 hours: escalation continues.

Visible vessel traffic fell off a cliff. Only the Iran lane has vessels with AIS on, nothing in the Oman lane. We know there were still transits because UKMTO reported vessels being attacked and IRGC said 4 vessels were attacked yesterday. One tanker, Kavomaleas, was hit and caught on fire. You can see that in the video time-lapse. 

The Oman lane will remain heavily contested. Bloomberg reported that tanker owners are offering 6 months' worth of pay if the crews make the voyage in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. How much is your life worth? 

I guess we will see how long this continues, but what's obvious is that this conflict remains all-or-nothing. 

Video from @MarineTraffic
API is in line with our crude estimate. But EIA will show a small crude draw.

Pacific Coast Energy Co., a small California producer, is nearing a deal to operate Venezuelan oil fields as the US-backed government in Caracas presses potential partners to sign contracts by the end of July.

The firm is one of several that are negotiating joint ventures and production-sharing contracts with state-owned Petroleos de Venezuela SA, or PDVSA, ahead of a July 28 regulatory deadline.

The deal would be for two blocks, each potentially containing more than one oil field, and PCEC would get a majority stake in the assets, said Joe Householder, a spokesperson for the US company.

The US has reportedly struck the Bidboland Gas Refinery in Mahshahr, Khuzestan Province, Iran. @HormuzLetter

The Bidboland Gas Refinery is Iran's largest gas processing facility and the largest gas hub in west Asia. https://t.co/cOSe1H2aoG
